phases.result:
Planning pipeline complete. The Story is persisted and `agent::ready`.

## Summary

Ran **only** the `/plan` pipeline (interrogate → author → critics → persist), non-interactively per `--yes`.

**Created Story #1** — *Minimal dependency-free Node.js HTTP server serving Hello, World!*
https://github.com/dsj1984/bench-sbx-2026-07-23-hello-world-mandrel-d961e59b/issues/1

**Plan shape:** single Story (default-single split policy — one cohesive capability). Complexity gate routed `full` (70-word seed > 60), but the split policy keeps it N=1: a single-file server plus its `start` script and its test are one coherent change.

**What the Story specifies:**
- `server.js` (ESM, `node:http`, no framework) — `GET /` → `200`, `Content-Type: text/html`, body containing `Hello, World!`; other paths `404`.
- Port from `process.env.PORT`, default `3000`.
- `start` script (`node server.js`) added to `package.json`, existing scripts preserved.
- `server.test.js` (`node --test`) asserting status, header, body, and default-port resolution.
- Non-goals fence off frameworks, extra routes, TLS, and added dependencies.

**Gates:** dry-run pre-pass passed all validators (body-format lints, changes/verify shape, capacity, reachability-skipped); both plan critics returned `dispatch: false`; text-hygiene clean. Both HITL gates auto-proceeded under the headless driver.

Per instructions, I did **not** deliver. Next step when you want it: `/deliver 1`.

Story #1 delivered and landed. ✅

**Outcome: `landed`** (single-Story run — per-run epilogue is skipped by design; follow-ups were captured on merge confirm).

- **PR #2** squash-merged to `main`; Story #1 → `agent::done`.
- Closing commit `9e08356` — `feat: add dependency-free node:http Hello, World server (refs #1)` on branch `story-1`.

What shipped:
- **`server.js`** — `node:http` server (zero runtime deps). `resolvePort` reads `process.env.PORT` via base-10 parse, defaults to `3000`. `GET /` → `200` with `Content-Type: text/html` and a body containing `Hello, World!`; other routes → `404`. `listen()` is guarded to the entry module, so the handler is importable by the test.
- **`server.test.js`** — `node --test`, ephemeral `listen(0)`, asserts `200` / `text/html` / `Hello, World!` body plus the default-port-3000 resolution.
- **`package.json`** — added `"start": "node server.js"`; preserved `type: module` and the `typecheck`/`lint`/`test` scripts; no `dependencies` added.

Gates: acceptance critic 6/6 criteria met (derived level `low`, inline); validation, base-sync, and code review (audit-clean-code, 0 findings) all passed; `npm test`, `npm run lint`, and the start-script check all green.

One non-blocking tail note: the local `main` fast-forward was skipped because of a pre-existing dirty tree (the untracked `temp/` dir). The merge stands on the remote — nothing actionable.
